forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred west of Tasmania extends a ridge over western Victoria. A cold front over the far west of SA will weaken as it moves eastwards over central and eastern parts this morning. The high will move eastwards to be centred southeast of Tasmania later today but will continue to extend a ridge over SA early next week. Another front will move across the south of SA late Tuesday and early Wednesday then another high will move across waters to the south during Wednesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers and possible thunderstorms moving across the agricultural area in the morning. Isolated showers persisting about southern coasts into the afternoon. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures above average in the west and south, but slightly below average in the northeast. Northeast to northwesterly winds, but southeasterly in the far northeast, ahead of a south to southeasterly change extending across the west and south.

Morning fog patches in the south. Dry apart from isolated morning showers about southern coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures above average in the west, but near or slightly below average in the east. Southeasterly winds tending east to northeasterly in the west.

Dry. Morning fog patches in the south. Maximum temperatures above average in the west and south, but near average in the northeast. Northeast to northwesterly winds, fresh at times in the west, shifting southwest to southeasterly in the far southwest. East to southeasterly winds in the far northeast.

text.p: Isolated light showers about southern coastal districts, mostly during the morning. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, but east to southeasterly in the far northeast, ahead of a south to southeasterly change extending across the west and south.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: up to 2 mm for the agricultural area and West Coast district, locally reaching 2 to 5 mm near southern coasts and ranges. Isolated falls of 5 to 10 mm are possible about the Lower South East.
